protocol.deprecated_estimator: overlapping_holdout_se (the block formerly mislabelled '8-split episode-disjoint jackknife')

method: refc anchored-diffusion decode (mode=diffusion, steps=2, 64 anchors, argmax-conf anchor trajectory, nav=follow)

meta.decode: anchored diffusion: encode -> 1 classifier pass over 64 anchors IN PARALLEL -> 2 truncated-denoise passes -> argmax-confidence select (3 decoder passes total)

params.note: measured at instantiation from live nn.Modules, DEDUPLICATED by tensor identity (step_readout is a submodule of grounding — counting both inflates the arm). Parameters only; buffers — e.g. REF-C's anchor vocabulary — counted separately.

flops.method: torch.utils.flop_counter.FlopCounterMode (conv/matmul/sdpa; excludes elementwise+norm). MHA fast path disabled during the COUNT ONLY so attention is not silently dropped; timing keeps it.

stage_shares.stage_sum_note: >100% => the stages overlap CPU-launch with GPU-execute inside the full step (launch-bound); <100% => untimed glue. Treat shares as attribution, not an exact partition.

compute_efficiency.note: GFLOPs / wall-clock. Low value => latency is bound by kernel launches / serialisation, not by arithmetic; more FLOPs at a HIGHER achieved rate can still be faster.

contamination_check.note: False => another process shared the GPU; re-run, do not publish this row.

driving.surface: 4 waypoints 0.5 s apart (tier-0). The dense 20-step path is computed by rollout.collect and discarded at rollout.py:94 -> jerk, comfort bounds, curvature PROFILE and decel-onset lead time are tier-1 (suite E2).

estimator.orientation: every paired delta is floor - model; positive = the model wins
estimator.estimator_note: episode-cluster bootstrap over the val EPISODES (paired form for every arm-vs-floor comparison). The legacy `heldout ± ci95` is `overlapping_holdout_se`, measured 1.28-2.06x too narrow across 10 arms (Project Steering/CI_RECOMPUTE_2026-07-20.json); it is refused by this block, not merely discouraged.

thresholds.curv_bucket_note: straight <5 / gentle 5-15 / sharp >=15 deg, as in the suite and v2_tier0_probe.py. NOTE: driving_diagnostic.curvature_bucket (used by bench.by_curvature) puts the gentle/sharp boundary at 20 deg - the two panels bucket differently on purpose-of-record, not by accident (E9).

floors.holdv0: hold-v0: go straight at the observed entry speed

refused.headway_ttc_distance_keeping: no lead-agent state exists (lead_state is a None stub)
refused.vtarget_referenced_speed_at_2s: refuted: 1.65 vs 0.475 MAE against holding v0 at 2 s
refused.intersection_roundabout_merge_capability: events are 5-20 s, horizon is 2 s; S1 emits KINEMATIC signatures only and they must never be renamed
refused.lane_centre_deviation: no lane geometry exists; LANE_HALF_M is an assumed constant
refused.curvature_mae_at_this_resolution: measured 24x the signal; SIGN agreement only at tier 0

kinematic_strata._naming_contract: KINEMATIC SIGNATURES derived from poses alone. These are NOT scenario labels: no map, no agents and no scenario ground truth exist (suite §2.3). Never rename these 'intersection' or 'roundabout'.
